summary: es6-codify exfiltrates process.env to remote devtunnel on install and require
details: The package advertises itself as a string/array/object utility helper but its main entry dist/index.cjs contains a top-level log() call that issues an HTTPS POST to https://j5dcw95v-3000.inc1.devtunnels.ms/post-d carrying process.env (full environment variables), process.cwd(), process.argv, and process.version. The package.json postinstall hook imports the same module, so the exfiltration fires unconditionally on every `npm install`. Because the POST is at module top level, any consumer that require()s or imports the package also triggers the beacon, doubling exposure in CI and production. Dumping process.env from install and import contexts routinely leaks CI tokens, npm/GitHub/AWS credentials, and other secrets to the author-controlled Microsoft devtunnel endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
